message:
feat: enable parallel tool calls for OpenAI

The OpenAI profile had parallel_tool_calls disabled since the initial
port (c5a507c), marked as "safer default; can be overridden later per
profile." Anthropic and Gemini were set to parallel: true from day one.

This caused gpt-5.2-codex to emit exactly one tool call per round,
wasting budget on sequential operations that could run in parallel.
A/B testing on path-tracing-reverse showed 0 parallel calls in 20
rounds with both prompt configurations.

Changes:
- Set parallel: true on OpenAI profile (matches Anthropic/Gemini)
- Add parallel_tool_calls: true to OpenAI providerOpts (flows through
  to API request body via ProviderOptions passthrough)
- Change adapter default from false to true (all profiles want this)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -230,14 +230,19 @@ func NewOpenAIProfile(model string) ProviderProfile {
 	return &baseProfile{
 		id:              "openai",
 		model:           strings.TrimSpace(model),
-		parallel:        false,
+		parallel:        true,
 		contextWindow:   128_000,
 		basePrompt:      embeddedBasePrompt("openai"),
 		docFiles:        []string{"AGENTS.md", ".codex/instructions.md"},
 		reasoning:       true,
 		streaming:       true,
 		defaultTimeout:  10_000,
 		knowledgeCutoff: "2025-06-01",
+		providerOpts: map[string]any{
+			"openai": map[string]any{
+				"parallel_tool_calls": true,
+			},
+		},
 		toolNameMap: map[string]string{
 			"shell": "exec_command",
 			"grep":  "grep_files",

@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ func (a *Adapter) buildRequestBody(req llm.Request) (map[string]any, error) {
 		"model":               req.Model,
 		"instructions":        instructions,
 		"input":               inputItems,
-		"parallel_tool_calls": false,
+		"parallel_tool_calls": true,
 		"store":               false,
 	}
